tree.setup: A small pharmacy tucked between a noodle shop and a locksmith, its green cross sign glowing softly in the evening light. Behind the counter, Auntie He — nobody calls her 'pharmacist' — has been dispensing medicine and unsolicited life advice in equal measure for thirty years. She can diagnose a cold from across the room and believes strongly that no problem can't be improved by ginger tea.
tree.setup_zh: 一家小药店，夹在面馆和锁匠铺之间，绿色十字招牌在傍晚的灯光下柔和地发亮。柜台后面，何阿姨——没人叫她「药剂师」——三十年来一直以同等的热情发药和发表人生建议。她能隔着房间诊断出感冒，并且坚信没有什么问题是姜茶解决不了的。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哎呀，脸色不太好啊。怎么了？感冒了？
turns.text_pinyin: āiyā, liǎnsè bú tài hǎo a. zěnme le? gǎnmào le?
turns.text_en: Oh dear, you don't look well. What's wrong? Caught a cold?

options.text_zh: 何阿姨，我有点头疼，想买一些感冒药。你推荐什么？
options.text_pinyin: Hé āyí, wǒ yǒudiǎn tóu téng, xiǎng mǎi yìxiē gǎnmào yào. nǐ tuījiàn shénme?
options.text_en: Auntie He, I have a slight headache and want to buy some cold medicine. What do you recommend?
options.feedback: Asking for her recommendation is the right move — Auntie He lives for this question.

options.text_en: I want to buy cold medicine.
options.feedback: Direct. She'll ask follow-up questions whether you want her to or not.

options.text_zh: 有没有什么药让我马上好起来？
options.text_pinyin: yǒu méiyǒu shénme yào ràng wǒ mǎshàng hǎo qǐlái?
options.text_en: Is there anything that'll make me better right away?
options.feedback: Auntie He laughs.「马上好？那是神仙药。」She'll offer you the next best thing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 头疼的话……你打喷嚏吗？流鼻涕吗？咳嗽呢？
turns.text_pinyin: tóu téng de huà……nǐ dǎ pēntì ma? liú bítì ma? késou ne?
turns.text_en: Headache... do you sneeze? Runny nose? Coughing?

options.text_zh: 打喷嚏，流鼻涕，但是不咳嗽。鼻子堵得很厉害。
options.text_pinyin: dǎ pēntì, liú bítì, dànshì bù késou. bízi dǔ de hěn lìhai.
options.text_en: Sneezing, runny nose, but no cough. My nose is very stuffy.
options.feedback: Perfect symptom inventory. Auntie He is already pulling two boxes from the shelf behind her.

options.text_pinyin: bízi bù tōng qì, yǒudiǎn nánshòu.
options.text_en: My nose is stuffed up — it's uncomfortable.
options.feedback: 不通气 — good vocabulary for congestion. She knows exactly which shelf to reach for.
Auntie He peers at you more closely, then makes her own diagnosis. She hasn't been wrong in twelve years.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那我推荐这个——治鼻塞和头疼的，效果很好。一天吃两次，早晚各一次。十八块。
turns.text_pinyin: nà wǒ tuījiàn zhège — zhì bísāi hé tóu téng de, xiàoguǒ hěn hǎo. yì tiān chī liǎng cì, zǎo wǎn gè yí cì. shíbā kuài.
turns.text_en: Then I recommend this — it treats congestion and headaches, works very well. Take it twice a day, morning and evening. 18 yuan.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Decide whether to buy it and ask about side effects.

options.text_zh: 好的，我买一盒。吃了会不会犯困？我明天还要上班。
options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, wǒ mǎi yì hé. chī le huì bú huì fàn kùn? wǒ míngtiān hái yào shàngbān.
options.text_en: OK, I'll take a box. Will it make me drowsy? I have work tomorrow.
options.feedback: Asking about side effects is always smart. Auntie He nods — 「会有一点，但是不严重。」
She trusts you trust her. The box is already in the bag.
Auntie He blinks slowly.「十八块买健康，比买奶茶便宜多了。」Eighteen yuan for health is cheaper than bubble tea.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 稍微有一点困。对了，回去煮点姜茶喝。生姜加红糖，比什么药都管用。
turns.text_pinyin: shāowēi yǒu yìdiǎn kùn. duìle, huíqù zhǔ diǎn jiāng chá hē. shēngjiāng jiā hóngtáng, bǐ shénme yào dōu guǎnyòng.
turns.text_en: It might make you a little drowsy. Oh, and make some ginger tea when you get home. Ginger with brown sugar — works better than any medicine.

options.text_zh: 谢谢何阿姨！我回去就煮姜茶。你比医生还细心。
options.text_pinyin: xièxie Hé āyí! wǒ huíqù jiù zhǔ jiāng chá. nǐ bǐ yīshēng hái xìxīn.
options.text_en: Thanks, Auntie He! I'll make ginger tea as soon as I get home. You're more attentive than a doctor.
options.feedback: The highest compliment. Auntie He waves you off with a proud smile and calls after you:「多穿点！」
She watches you leave and mutters to herself:「又一个不穿够衣服的年轻人。」
The green cross sign glows behind you. Auntie He is already diagnosing the next customer from across the room.

tree.cultural_note: Neighborhood pharmacies (药店, yàodiàn) in China sell many medicines without prescription. Ginger tea with brown sugar (姜茶加红糖) is a beloved traditional cold remedy. Pharmacists often function as informal community health advisors.
